definition: Private-sector GDP growth stripped of fiscal demand contribution is only ~1.0-1.5%, near or below stall speed. This is derived mechanically from headline GDP minus the fiscal contribution and implies the economy would be at or below stall speed without the deficit.
evidence: Multiple agents arrive at the same ~1.0-1.5% figure independently using different frameworks
Consistent with leading indicator signals detecting genuine private-sector weakness
Yield curve, LEI, and Sahm Rule readings align with this private-sector growth rate
open_questions: Is the fiscal-adjusted growth rate stable or deteriorating over time?
How does the elevated stall speed (1.5-2.0% via EBITDA addback arithmetic) interact with this reading?
